claim: Anthropic must destroy pirated book datasets within 30 days of final judgment per a $1.5B copyright settlement, but Claude models remain trained on that data
actionable: Understand that model fine-tuning on infringing data cannot be reversed; future regulation will likely focus on data provenance attestation

claim: The copyright settlement release extends only to past claims and does not cover future reproduction, derivative works, or model output claims
actionable: Be aware that settling past infringement does not prevent future copyright suits if models reproduce works near-verbatim

claim: AI companies will face significantly increased costs if fair use arguments fail; licensed data will become much more valuable and expensive
actionable: For data-dependent AI products, plan for higher data acquisition costs; secure long-term licensing agreements with publishers and content creators

claim: Anthropic intentionally downloaded pirated books thinking it was fair use, but the court ruled it inherently and irredeemably infringing
actionable: Do not assume downloading pirated datasets constitutes fair use; obtain explicit licenses for training data or use public domain sources

claim: The New York Times lawsuit against OpenAI for similar copyright infringement is still pending and may set additional precedent
actionable: Monitor ongoing copyright litigation against major LLM providers; future rulings may strengthen copyright enforcement and licensing requirements
